articles.title: Fed Holds Rates Steady, Signals Two Cuts Later This Year
articles.summary: The Federal Reserve held its benchmark rate at 5.25–5.50 % for a fifth consecutive meeting. Chair Powell indicated policymakers still expect two quarter-point reductions before year-end, conditional on inflation continuing its downward path. Markets reacted calmly, with the 10-year Treasury yield edging 4 bps lower to 4.32 %.

articles.title: NVIDIA Reports Record Q1 Revenue of $36 Billion, Beats Estimates
articles.summary: NVIDIA posted first-quarter revenue of $36 B, up 78 % year-over-year, driven by insatiable demand for its H100 and Blackwell GPU clusters. Data-centre revenue hit $32.6 B. Management guided Q2 revenue to approximately $38 B (±2 %), well above consensus at $35.5 B. Shares rose 6 % in after-hours trading.

articles.title: Bitcoin Breaks $95,000 as Institutional Demand Surges
articles.summary: Bitcoin briefly topped $95,000 for the first time since January, buoyed by record weekly inflows into spot BTC ETFs ($2.1 B). Analysts cite a favourable macro backdrop—declining DXY and falling real yields—as tailwinds. On-chain data shows long-term holder supply near all-time highs, reducing near-term sell pressure.

articles.title: Oil Slides 3 % After OPEC+ Agrees to Accelerate Production Ramp-Up
articles.summary: Brent crude fell to $78.40/bbl after OPEC+ members voted to add 411,000 bpd in July—faster than the original tapering schedule. Saudi Arabia signalled willingness to tolerate lower prices to regain market share. Energy stocks underperformed; XOM and CVX each shed ~2 % intraday.

articles.title: Apple Plans AI-Powered Siri Overhaul at WWDC 2026
articles.summary: Apple is expected to unveil a fully re-engineered Siri powered by a proprietary on-device LLM at WWDC in June. Leaks suggest deep integration with iOS 20 apps, real-time translation, and a new developer API. Analysts expect the upgrade to drive iPhone 17 upgrade cycles and potentially challenge OpenAI's consumer AI dominance.

articles.title: ECB Cuts Rates by 25 bps, Euro Falls to 1.07 Against Dollar
articles.summary: The European Central Bank cut its deposit facility rate to 2.75 %, as expected, citing weak Eurozone growth and receding inflation. ECB President Lagarde left the door open for further easing. EUR/USD dropped 0.8 % to 1.07 on the announcement. European bond yields also dipped across the board.
